title: 'Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker' Emergency Deep Dive (SPOILERS) | The Big Picture
format: Sean Fennessey and Mallory Rubin, one viewing deep, process the end of the Skywalker saga the morning after seeing it.

segments.heading: How do you end a saga?

films.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
films.note: The gold standard for sticking a trilogy's landing, though the hosts note it had a source text to work from that Star Wars doesn't.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2
films.note: Another ending they call satisfying, with a side gripe about splitting the last book into two movies.

films.title: Ocean's Thirteen
films.note: Cited as a trilogy that closed itself out well.

films.title: Ocean's Eight
films.note: The proof the well can still be revisited without ruining the original ending.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Called the real winner of the week: the same job as 'Rise of Skywalker,' closing out a sprawling, multi-filmmaker saga, done with a coherence Star Wars couldn't manage.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: J.J. Abrams closes the sequel trilogy by reviving Palpatine with no real explanation and making Rey his granddaughter, a twist the hosts can't fully get behind even as they try to talk themselves into it. They fault the breakneck pace, fake-out deaths (Chewbacca survives an explosion minutes after 'dying,' Kylo Ren gets resurrected) that drain the stakes, and a script visibly reverse-engineered around the Carrie Fisher footage available after her death. What works: Rey and Kylo Ren's force-bond scenes, Adam Driver and Daisy Ridley's performances, and Rey igniting her own yellow lightsaber at the end.

films: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace
Star Wars: Episode II - Attack of the Clones
